reasoning: Recent data shows a clear short-term downward trend, falling from a peak of 1,962,000 in late October to 1,932,000 by Nov 22. This rapid 30k decline reflects a normalization from previous short-term economic disruptions (such as strikes washing out). The strong negative momentum over the last four weeks indicates that in the immediate term (Nov 30, 1 week away), continued claims will likely keep dropping as this normalization completes. In addition, the short-term Thanksgiving holiday week often adds downward pressure due to reporting delays. Thus, the probability of an increase by Nov 30 is significantly below 0.5. Looking 1-month out (late December), the washout effect will have stabilized, making an increase more uncertain but still leaning lower relative to the baseline. For the 3-month forecast (February), unpredictability dominates; per the guidance to increase uncertainty over the forecast horizon, the probability converges much closer to 0.5.
